topics.name: Ordering Numbers
topics.description: Order positive and negative integers, decimals and fractions; use the symbols =, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥

topics.name: Four Operations
topics.description: Add, subtract, multiply and divide integers, decimals and fractions; mixed numbers; place value; financial terms

topics.name: Order of Operations
topics.description: Use brackets, powers, roots and reciprocals; understand and use the conventions of BIDMAS/BODMAS

topics.name: Prime Numbers, Factors & Multiples
topics.description: Prime numbers, factors, multiples, HCF, LCM, prime factorisation, product notation

topics.name: Systematic Listing
topics.description: Systematic listing strategies including the product rule for counting

topics.name: Powers and Roots
topics.description: Use positive integer powers and roots; square, cube and higher; recognise powers of 2, 3, 4, 5; estimate powers and roots

topics.name: Integer Indices
topics.description: Calculate with integer indices; negative and fractional indices (Higher)

topics.name: Surds
topics.description: Calculate exactly with fractions, multiples of π; simplify surds, rationalise denominators

topics.name: Standard Form
topics.description: Calculate with numbers in standard form A × 10^n where 1 ≤ A < 10

topics.name: Terminating & Recurring Decimals
topics.description: Convert between terminating decimals and fractions; recurring decimals to fractions (Higher)

topics.name: Fractions in Ratio Problems
topics.description: Work with fractions in ratio problems

topics.name: Fractions & Percentages as Operators
topics.description: Interpret fractions and percentages as operators

topics.name: Units & Conversions
topics.description: Use standard units of measure; compound measures; metric/imperial conversions

topics.name: Estimation & Approximation
topics.description: Estimate calculations; check results using approximation

topics.name: Rounding & Error Intervals
topics.description: Round to decimal places and significant figures; use error intervals from truncation and rounding

topics.name: Upper and Lower Bounds
topics.description: Calculate upper and lower bounds; use bounds in calculations

topics.name: Algebraic Notation
topics.description: Use and interpret algebraic notation: ab, 3y, a², a³, a/b, brackets

topics.name: Substitution
topics.description: Substitute numerical values into formulae and expressions

topics.name: Algebraic Terminology
topics.description: Understand expressions, equations, formulae, identities, inequalities, terms, factors

topics.name: Simplifying Expressions
topics.description: Simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions: collecting like terms, expanding brackets, factorising

topics.name: Rearranging Formulae
topics.description: Use standard mathematical formulae; rearrange formulae to change the subject

topics.name: Algebraic Proof
topics.description: Know the difference between an equation and an identity; argue mathematically to show algebraic expressions are equivalent

topics.name: Functions
topics.description: Work with functions and their inverses; composite functions; f(x), fg(x), f⁻¹(x) notation

topics.name: Coordinates
topics.description: Work with coordinates in all four quadrants

topics.name: Straight Line Graphs
topics.description: Plot graphs of equations; y = mx + c; find equation of line through two points; parallel and perpendicular lines (Higher)

topics.name: Gradients & Intercepts
topics.description: Identify and interpret gradients and intercepts of linear functions graphically and algebraically

topics.name: Quadratic Graphs
topics.description: Identify roots, intercepts and turning points of quadratic functions; completing the square (Higher)

topics.name: Other Graphs
topics.description: Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of linear, quadratic, cubic, reciprocal, exponential and trigonometric functions

topics.name: Graph Transformations
topics.description: Sketch translations and reflections of a given function

topics.name: Real-Life Graphs
topics.description: Plot and interpret graphs including reciprocal and non-standard functions in real contexts

topics.name: Gradients & Areas Under Graphs
topics.description: Calculate or estimate gradients and areas under graphs; interpret in distance-time and velocity-time contexts

topics.name: Circles
topics.description: Recognise and use the equation of a circle with centre at the origin; find equation of tangent at a point

topics.name: Solving Linear Equations
topics.description: Solve linear equations in one unknown; include unknown on both sides, brackets, fractions

topics.name: Solving Quadratic Equations
topics.description: Solve quadratic equations algebraically by factorising; completing the square and quadratic formula (Higher); solve graphically

topics.name: Simultaneous Equations
topics.description: Solve two simultaneous equations; linear/linear and linear/quadratic (Higher)

topics.name: Iteration
topics.description: Find approximate solutions using systematic iteration; use suffix notation in recursive formulae

topics.name: Modelling with Algebra
topics.description: Translate simple situations into algebraic expressions or formulae; derive and solve equations

topics.name: Inequalities
topics.description: Solve linear inequalities; represent solutions on number line and using set notation; quadratic inequalities (Higher)

topics.name: Generating Sequences
topics.description: Generate terms of a sequence from term-to-term or position-to-term rules

topics.name: Types of Sequences
topics.description: Recognise and use sequences: triangular, square, cube, arithmetic, geometric, Fibonacci-type, quadratic (Higher)

topics.name: nth Term
topics.description: Find the nth term of linear sequences; quadratic sequences (Higher)

topics.name: Compound Units
topics.description: Convert between standard units; use compound units: speed, rates of pay, prices, density, pressure

topics.name: Scale Drawings
topics.description: Use scale factors, scale diagrams and maps

topics.name: Fractions of Amounts
topics.description: Express one quantity as a fraction of another

topics.name: Simplifying Ratio
topics.description: Use ratio notation; reduce ratios to simplest form

topics.name: Dividing in a Ratio
topics.description: Divide a given quantity into two parts in a given part:part or part:whole ratio

topics.name: Multiplicative Relationships
topics.description: Express a multiplicative relationship between two quantities as a ratio or a fraction

topics.name: Proportion
topics.description: Understand proportion as equality of ratios; relate ratios to fractions

topics.name: Ratio & Linear Functions
topics.description: Relate ratios to fractions and linear functions; represent ratios graphically

topics.name: Percentages
topics.description: Define percentage; interpret percentages and percentage changes; percentage increase/decrease; original value problems; compound interest

topics.name: Direct & Inverse Proportion
topics.description: Solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion; graphical and algebraic representations

topics.name: Compound Measures
topics.description: Compare lengths, areas and volumes using ratio notation and scale factors; make links to similarity

topics.name: Similarity & Trigonometry
topics.description: Understand that X is inversely proportional to Y is equivalent to X is proportional to 1/Y

topics.name: Equations of Proportion
topics.description: Interpret equations that describe direct and inverse proportion

topics.name: Graphs & Rates of Change
topics.description: Interpret the gradient of a straight line graph as a rate of change

topics.name: Instantaneous Rate of Change
topics.description: Calculate the instantaneous rate of change by drawing or estimating a tangent

topics.name: Growth & Decay
topics.description: Set up, solve and interpret answers in growth and decay problems; compound interest

topics.name: Geometric Notation
topics.description: Use conventional terms and notation: points, lines, vertices, edges, planes, parallel, perpendicular, right angles, polygons, regular polygons

topics.name: Constructions
topics.description: Use the standard ruler and compass constructions; perpendicular bisector, angle bisector, constructing triangles

topics.name: Angles
topics.description: Apply angle properties at a point, on a straight line, vertically opposite; parallel lines; angles in triangles and polygons

topics.name: Quadrilaterals & Triangles
topics.description: Apply the properties and definitions of quadrilaterals and triangles

topics.name: Congruence Criteria
topics.description: Know and use the criteria for congruent triangles: SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS

topics.name: Geometric Proof
topics.description: Apply angle facts, triangle congruence, similarity and properties of quadrilaterals to conjecture and derive results

topics.name: Transformations
topics.description: Identify, describe and construct congruent and similar shapes; rotation, reflection, translation, enlargement; fractional and negative scale factors

topics.name: Combined Transformations
topics.description: Describe the changes and invariance achieved by combinations of transformations

topics.name: Circle Parts
topics.description: Identify and apply definitions of circle: centre, radius, chord, diameter, circumference, tangent, arc, sector, segment

topics.name: Circle Theorems
topics.description: Apply and prove circle theorems: angle at centre, angle in semicircle, angles in same segment, cyclic quadrilateral, tangent properties, alternate segment

topics.name: Coordinate Geometry Problems
topics.description: Solve geometrical problems on coordinate axes

topics.name: 3D Shapes
topics.description: Identify properties of cuboids, prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, spheres; faces, surfaces, edges, vertices

topics.name: Plans & Elevations
topics.description: Construct and interpret plans and elevations of 3D shapes

topics.name: Units of Measure
topics.description: Use standard units of measure and related concepts; length, area, volume, capacity, mass, time, money

topics.name: Measuring & Bearings
topics.description: Measure line segments and angles; use bearings to specify direction

topics.name: Area & Volume
topics.description: Know and apply formulae to calculate area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezia; volume of cuboids and prisms

topics.name: Circles: Area & Circumference
topics.description: Calculate circumference and area of circles; arcs and sectors; surface area and volume of spheres, pyramids, cones (Higher)

topics.name: Arcs & Sectors
topics.description: Calculate arc lengths, angles and areas of sectors of circles

topics.name: Similar Shapes
topics.description: Compare the areas and volumes of similar shapes; apply relationships between lengths, areas and volumes in similar figures

topics.name: Pythagoras & Trigonometry
topics.description: Know and use Pythagoras' theorem; trigonometric ratios in right-angled triangles; use these in 3D (Higher)

topics.name: Exact Trigonometric Values
topics.description: Know the exact values of sin θ and cos θ for θ = 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°; know exact value of tan θ for θ = 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°

topics.name: Sine & Cosine Rules
topics.description: Know and apply the sine rule and cosine rule to find unknown lengths and angles

topics.name: Area of Triangle Formula
topics.description: Know and use the formula: Area = ½ab sin C

topics.name: Vectors
topics.description: Describe translations as 2D vectors; apply addition and subtraction of vectors; scalar multiplication

topics.name: Vector Proofs
topics.description: Use vectors to construct geometric arguments and proofs

topics.name: Recording Outcomes
topics.description: Record, describe and analyse the frequency of outcomes of simple probability experiments

topics.name: Fairness & Randomness
topics.description: Understand that the probabilities of all possible outcomes sum to 1; randomness, fairness, equally likely events

topics.name: Probability Scale
topics.description: Understand the 0-1 probability scale; use relative frequency and theoretical probability

topics.name: Mutually Exclusive Events
topics.description: Enumerate sets and combinations of sets systematically; mutually exclusive events; probability sums to 1

topics.name: Experimental vs Theoretical
topics.description: Understand that empirical samples tend towards theoretical probability

topics.name: Systematic Listing
topics.description: Enumerate sets and combinations of sets; Venn diagrams; systematic listing strategies

topics.name: Possibility Spaces
topics.description: Construct and use possibility spaces for single and combined experiments

topics.name: Tree Diagrams
topics.description: Calculate the probability of independent and dependent combined events using tree diagrams

topics.name: Conditional Probability
topics.description: Calculate and interpret conditional probabilities; use two-way tables, tree diagrams and Venn diagrams

topics.name: Sampling
topics.description: Infer properties of populations from samples; understand limitations of sampling

topics.name: Charts & Diagrams
topics.description: Interpret and construct: tables, bar charts, pie charts, pictograms, vertical line charts, time series

topics.name: Grouped Data
topics.description: Interpret and construct diagrams for grouped data: histograms with equal/unequal class intervals, cumulative frequency graphs

topics.name: Measures of Central Tendency
topics.description: Interpret, analyse and compare distributions: median, mean, mode, modal class; range, quartiles, interquartile range (Higher)

topics.name: Describing Populations
topics.description: Apply statistics to describe a population

topics.name: Scatter Graphs
topics.description: Use and interpret scatter graphs; correlation; lines of best fit; interpolation and extrapolation

studentsMustMemorise: Area of triangle = ½ × base × height
Area of trapezium = ½(a + b) × height
providedInExam: Volume of prism = area of cross-section × length
Surface area of cone = πrl + πr² (Higher)
Sine rule: a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C (Higher)
Cosine rule: a² = b² + c² - 2bc cos A (Higher)
Area of triangle = ½ab sin C (Higher)

notes.contentIdentical: All exam boards use identical subject content (DfE mandated). Differences are in paper structure and question style only.
notes.foundationHigher: Foundation tier covers grades 1-5, Higher covers grades 4-9. Higher tier includes all Foundation content plus additional topics.
notes.calculatorPapers: All boards have one non-calculator paper. AQA/Edexcel: Paper 1; OCR: Paper 2/5; Eduqas: Paper 1.
